# Test basic client-server interaction using mcpscripttest infrastructure

# Start a simple MCP echo server in the background
mcp-server-start echo-server go run examples/servers/mcp-echo-server/main.go

# Wait for server to be ready
[mcp_server_running]

# Send initialization request
mcp-server-send echo-server '{"jsonrpc": "2.0", "id": 1, "method": "initialize", "params": {"protocolVersion": "2024-11-05", "capabilities": {"tools": {}}, "clientInfo": {"name": "test-client", "version": "1.0.0"}}}'

# Verify response contains server capabilities
stdout '2024-11-05'
stdout 'capabilities'

# Send initialized notification
mcp-server-send echo-server '{"jsonrpc": "2.0", "method": "notifications/initialized"}'

# List available tools
mcp-server-send echo-server '{"jsonrpc": "2.0", "id": 2, "method": "tools/list"}'

# Verify echo tool is available
stdout 'echo'
stdout 'result'

# Call the echo tool
mcp-server-send echo-server '{"jsonrpc": "2.0", "id": 3, "method": "tools/call", "params": {"name": "echo", "arguments": {"message": "Hello, World!"}}}'

# Verify echo response
stdout 'Hello, World!'

# Stop the server
mcp-server-stop echo-server
